questions.question: Q1. What is the primary purpose of a smart grid?

answers.a: To improve energy generation and distribution efficiency

explanations.a: Smart grids use digital technologies to improve the efficiency, reliability, and sustainability of electricity distribution.
explanations.b: Incorrect. Smart grids aim to optimize consumption, not increase it.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Smart grids work with existing power plants.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Smart grids still use electrical transmission infrastructure.

questions.question: Q2. Which component generates electrical power in a smart grid system?

explanations.a: Consumers use electricity rather than generate it.
explanations.c: Power plants generate electrical energy for distribution.

questions.question: Q3. What does load balancing in a smart grid help prevent?

explanations.a: Load balancing does not prevent generation.
explanations.b: Correct. It distributes demand evenly and prevents overload conditions.
explanations.d: Data transmission is not the primary goal.

questions.question: Q4. Which of the following is a major advantage of smart grids?

explanations.b: Smart grids rely on automated monitoring.
explanations.c: Correct. Real-time monitoring improves reliability and efficiency.
explanations.d: Smart grids aim to reduce losses.

questions.question: Q5. What device measures electricity usage in a smart grid?

explanations.b: Correct. Smart meters record and communicate electricity usage data.

questions.question: Q6. What happens when total energy demand exceeds supply?

explanations.a: Correct. Excess demand can overload the grid.

questions.question: Q7. Which sector typically has the highest electricity demand?

explanations.a: Residential demand is generally lower than industrial demand.
explanations.b: Correct. Industrial facilities often consume the most electricity.
explanations.c: Street lighting uses less power overall.
explanations.d: Telecommunication systems use comparatively less energy.

questions.question: Q8. What is the role of renewable energy in a smart grid?

explanations.c: Correct. Renewable sources support cleaner energy generation.
explanations.d: Renewable energy has no relation to disabling smart meters.

questions.question: Q9. Which parameter is commonly monitored in a smart grid?

explanations.a: Correct. Monitoring demand helps optimize distribution.
explanations.d: Weather may influence demand but is not the primary parameter.

questions.question: Q10. Why is efficiency important in energy distribution?

answers.c: To deliver maximum energy with minimum losses

explanations.c: Correct. Efficient systems maximize delivered energy and minimize losses.
explanations.d: Efficiency does not necessarily reduce capacity.
